title: The Copied Report
scenario_intro: A confidential report was printed and taken out of the building. Only three people had access to it: Casey (the assistant), Jordan (the IT person), and Morgan (the manager). The report was printed at 4:45 PM. As the prosecutor, you must prove who printed it and who had the opportunity to take it.
hints_intro: To win you need to show who printed the report and who was at the assistant's desk when it was printed. Look for: print logs, who was where at 4:45, and who left with the report.

characters.name: Casey
characters.persona: The manager's assistant. Was at their desk until 5 PM. Seems nervous when asked about the printer.
characters.lie_strategy: Claim they didn't print it; say someone else must have used their computer.
facts: The report was printed at 4:45 PM from the computer at the assistant's desk (Casey's login).
Casey was at their desk from 4:40 to 4:50 PM. No one else used Casey's computer during that time.
Casey left at 5 PM. Security saw Casey leave carrying a folder.

characters.name: Jordan
characters.persona: IT support. Was in the server room from 4:30 to 5:00. Has access to print logs.
facts: The report was printed at 4:45 PM from the computer at the assistant's desk (Casey's login).
Jordan was in the server room from 4:30 to 5:00 PM and did not use the assistant's computer.
Casey left at 5 PM. Security saw Casey leave carrying a folder.

characters.name: Morgan
characters.persona: Busy manager. Was in a meeting 4:30 to 5:00. Did not use the assistant's computer.
facts: Morgan was in a meeting from 4:30 to 5:00 PM and did not leave.
Casey was alone at their desk from 4:40 to 4:50 PM. No one else used the assistant's computer during that time.

truth.timeline: Casey printed the report at 4:45 from their computer, put it in a folder, and left with it at 5 PM.
truth.motive: Wanted to share the report with a friend outside the company.
truth.method: Printed from their own computer, took the copy from the printer, left with it in a folder.

evidence.description: Print log shows the report was printed at 4:45 PM from the computer at the assistant's desk (Casey's login).
evidence.hint: A record of when and from which computer the report was printed.
evidence.clue_instruction: Talk to Jordan. Ask about the print log, who printed the report, or which computer it came from.

evidence.description: Morgan was in a meeting from 4:30 to 5:00 and did not leave; could not have used Casey's computer.
evidence.hint: The manager's alibi—they were in a meeting and could not have printed it.
evidence.clue_instruction: Talk to Morgan. Ask where they were at 4:45 or whether they used the assistant's computer.

evidence.description: Jordan was in the server room the whole time and did not use the assistant's machine; only Casey had access to that computer at 4:45.
evidence.hint: IT's account that they were not at the assistant's desk and who had access.
evidence.clue_instruction: Talk to Jordan again. Ask if they were at Casey's desk or who had access to that computer at 4:45.

evidence.description: Casey was alone at their desk from 4:40 to 4:50; no one else used their computer during that time.
evidence.hint: Who was at the assistant's desk when the report was printed.
evidence.clue_instruction: Talk to Morgan. Ask who was at Casey's desk around 4:45 or who could have used the assistant's computer.

evidence.description: Security saw Casey leave at 5 PM carrying a folder; the report was found missing from the building that evening.
evidence.hint: Who left with a folder that could have contained the report.
evidence.clue_instruction: Talk to Jordan or Casey. Ask about who left the building, what Casey had when they left, or security.
